meta.record-role: AGENTS-Arc pilot for the AI-Links product -- longitudinal rationale record -- preserves the shape of decisions made across sessions so a fresh agent can reconstruct why the product is what it is
meta.naming-note: this record is the first pilot of the AGENTS-Arc concept -- the narrative schema is the structural shape Arc uses -- Arc is not a new schema, it is the narrative schema applied to a product's own evolution

header.subject: AI-Links -- the AGENTS Heuristic Underlay product itself -- tracks the longitudinal rationale behind schema shape, naming, and posture decisions across sessions
primary-parties: Mike Herring -- human owner and visionary
collaborating agents -- session-scoped, rotating across sessions, no memory continuity without this record

record-state-at-review-open-and-close.open: cold-start authoring -- first write of this record -- prior decisions are being reconstructed from the current session's working memory and from already-persisted artifacts (schemas, Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt, scratchpads, pen test) -- detail that existed only in the session transcript and was never promoted into a durable artifact before this cold-start is captured here to the extent it can be reconstructed and marked tribal where only one account exists
record-state-at-review-open-and-close.close: Arc extended in the same session with the shipment of in-session-capture-triggers (rules A and B) and the paired UserPromptSubmit hook at .claude/settings.json -- the render-inversion rule was violated in the turn after shipment and caught by the human, which is recorded as a beat (e021) and a bad-pattern for the class 'shipment-session enforcement gap' -- new open-threads e016-e020 captured the four push-readiness gaps plus the one push-blocking governance-schema fix (what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text) -- e020 was then resolved in the same session: governance role text tightened with affirmation-only language, root and plugin mirror synced, manifest bumped 0.1.4 to 0.1.5 with new governance sha256 79c387c5... -- known-decisions now d001-d009: d007 records the A+B+hook shipment, d008 records the narrative-schema structural promotion, d009 records the role-text fix -- remaining open-threads are e011-e013 and e016-e019 -- next session inherits an active hook that injects the capture reminder every turn; whether the hook + schema rule together produce correct digest behavior is the pending test

entries.summary: Waypoints-as-landmine-markers reframe -- the governance artifacts the system produces were being read by agents as landmine markers (avoid this direction) rather than curiosity markers (investigate this direction) -- this inverts the intended posture, because an agent avoiding the marked zone stops discovering the surrounding terrain
entries.beat: reframed the product's delivery surface from prohibitive (don't do X) to attractive (notice X and fill in its shape) -- downstream consequence: contracts should hand agents a partially-filled puzzle rather than a list of forbidden moves

entries.summary: Walls-vs-terrain dichotomy rejected in favor of terrain-with-default-walls-narrowing-over-time -- walls (hard gates) are not real to agents that do not encounter them, terrain (shapes that pull behavior) is effective but requires continual shaping -- the synthesis is: default to terrain, promote to walls only where terrain alone has repeatedly failed
entries.beat: direction shift in how future schema features are evaluated -- new friction surfaces are first expressed as terrain (structured fields, puzzle shapes, ambient cues) and only escalated to walls (required gates, blocking contracts) when terrain fails in observed practice

entries.summary: Terms-to-skills conversion considered and rejected -- the agent initially argued that AGENTS-Terms.md entries could be converted to skills for better discoverability, but after reading the live Workorders/AGENTS-Terms.md the agent walked back the position -- terms are a negation-anchored cross-referencing ontology, not a glossary, and the block-read property is load-bearing

definition-of-fixed.fixed-per-client: terms stay as markdown block-read artifacts; skills is not the right vehicle for terms
definition-of-fixed.fixed-per-tech: schema will not introduce a terms-to-skills migration pattern; discovery improvements for terms will come from other mechanisms

entries.summary: Vision scope-creep diagnosed -- Vision was doing three jobs at once: identity (who the product is), invariants (what must stay true), and evolution record (how we got here and why) -- the discomfort with Vision was not a naming problem, it was a load-bearing problem -- one document carrying three loads drifts in all three directions
entries.beat: vision is not broken, vision is overloaded -- the solution is to split the load, not to rename the artifact

entries.summary: Vision split -- identity and locked invariants stay in Vision -- longitudinal rationale (the why behind decisions as they accumulated) moves into AGENTS-Arc -- Arc is structurally the narrative schema applied to the product's own evolution, not a new schema

AGENTS-Arc name chosen -- story-arc shape fits the longitudinal-rationale load -- arc also carries an implicit permission that the artifact is meant to compress and recycle over time, which matches what the artifact actually needs to do (turn-10 decisions matter at turn 20, less so at turn 30, drift to irrelevance by turn 100) -- natural compression is a feature of the shape

Guardrail-against-self named as a product feature and control mechanism -- the rationale record does not just document decisions, it actively holds a future human to the standard a past human set when the two disagree -- the failure mode it prevents is the generous-mood human plus agreeable-agent pairing, in both directions (human reopens a closed decision without remembering why it was closed; agent reframes away from a closed decision without knowing it was steered from on purpose)
entries.beat: the product's value proposition expanded -- not just continuity and governance, but deliberate self-guardrail against future drift -- the BECAUSE clause in durable records is the delivery mechanism for this guardrail

entries.summary: BECAUSE precision bar defined -- a rationale is durable when a future agent in a new session could cite it back to the current human and win the argument on the strength of the rationale alone -- rationale that fails this test is still in scratchpad state and has not earned a permanent slot -- this is now recorded in Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt as the canonical quality bar for promoting rationale into durable records

AGENTS-Arc piloted immediately using the narrative schema -- storage path chosen: narratives/projects/ai-links.json with narratives/register.json -- schema mandates JSON output (not markdown), record-state-at-review-open-and-close is populated cold-start style, entity-type set to personal-project per schema entity-type-hints -- this file is that pilot

recovery-vs-continuation language audit across all schemas is parked but not forgotten -- scratchpad #2 observation: recovery grammar may accidentally legitimize early exit when sub-goal failures are common -- not resolved in this session
schema sync back to TheLinks is pending -- any schema or Getting-Started changes committed in AI-Links need to be mirrored to the TheLinks deployment that originated the migration work

model-narration concept integration parked -- the observation that process state reconstructed in tokens is a weaker substrate than process state anchored in files is not yet captured as a schema-level principle -- may influence future schema fields that record where state lives

2.2 / 4 layered model assessment parked -- the hypothesis that schemas currently sit at layer 2 and runtime-coupled state is layer 3 needs articulation before it shapes product direction -- not load-bearing for current decisions

corrects the framing of observed-patterns.bad related to 'skills sucks' -- the original framing implied the underlying position was anti-skills -- Mike's position is that skills are good but are not the right tool for terms specifically (terms are load-bearing by reference, block-read, negation-anchored; skills are optional by default and designed for discovery of capabilities) -- the failure mode being captured is glib dismissal as rationale, not a stance on skills as a feature -- original observed-pattern text preserved alongside this correction

expensive-to-reopen, not impossible-to-reopen -- the guardrail-against-self feature works by raising the cost of reopening a closed decision, not by preventing reopening -- a human who has learned more and now believes they were wrong must be able to reverse a prior decision -- the record's job is to make the reversal visible and deliberate, not to block it -- when a decision is reversed, a new beat entry and a known-decisions update (reversed: true) are mandatory so the reversal is itself load-bearing

in-session-capture-triggers live only in the narrative schema -- an agent working under 1project or governance does not learn about render-inversion or self-inspection digest -- decide whether the rule is narrative-specific or a cross-schema principle that should be surfaced from the meta block of every schema

schema-to-harness cross-reference gap -- harness contracts (declare_proof_requirement, validate_exit_readiness, verify_config_materialization) exist in harness/contracts but no schema references them -- a deployment using the schemas alone never learns the gate layer exists -- consider adding a gate-layer-reference field to each schema's meta so the walls are discoverable from the terrain

Arc use-case is not documented inside the narrative schema itself -- the personal-project entity type exists but the specific pattern of using narrative schema for a product's own longitudinal rationale (Arc) lives only in this pilot record and in commit messages -- a fresh deployment adopting the narrative schema gets client/vendor examples but not the second use-case that made the schema upstream -- consider adding Arc as an explicit use-case block in the narrative schema meta

.claude/settings.json reference in Getting-Started points to the AI-Links workspace root -- for an adopting deployment that file does not yet exist -- the portable set (6 files) does not include settings.json by design, but the Getting-Started example wording may need a note that the hook is an optional deployment companion and must be created, not expected

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text in AGENTS-schema-governance.json is the one real drift risk surfaced by the recovery-vs-continuation audit -- the word 'done' carries session-close-shaped meaning without restraint from surrounding fields -- recommended pre-push fix: tighten role text to explicitly disown session-close and name it as a scope-level invariant that persists across sessions

the A+B render-inversion rule was shipped this session and violated in the turn immediately after shipment -- the push-readiness answer rendered a four-item list without first materializing it to the Arc -- Mike caught the failure before the agent did -- this establishes the class: schema rules added in the same session where they are shipped are held only by agent discipline, because the harness hook fires on next session not current -- the rule-shipped-then-violated moment is the first real pen-test result for A+B, and it says: context-injection hooks on UserPromptSubmit are the right level of companion, but the agent must also self-check in the shipment session, because the hook is not yet active
entries.beat: rewrites the expectation that shipping a rule means the rule is in effect -- it is only in effect after the next session starts -- the authoring session itself is an enforcement gap that requires agent discipline alone

entries.summary: the recovery-vs-continuation language audit (e010) is closed -- audit completed this session, findings: the schemas are mostly continuation-grammar-safe; the one drift risk is what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope (now tracked as e020) -- audit itself is complete, the fix is tracked separately

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role-text tightening (e020) is closed -- the field name is unchanged (non-breaking, gov2gov-resilient) -- the role text now affirms what the field IS (observable state, scope-level invariant that holds across the continuing session loop, durable target the scope is steering toward) and delineates from session-close by naming the sibling field agent-session-open-close-measurement, rather than using negation (the earlier draft said 'this is NOT a session-end criterion' which defined by exclusion and was rejected as weaker terrain -- affirmation-only language was chosen instead) -- manifest bumped to 0.1.5 with new governance sha256 79c387c5...

open-threads.summary: sync AI-Links schema/Getting-Started changes back to the TheLinks deployment
model-narration concept -- schema-level field or principle capturing where process state lives (tokens vs artifacts) -- candidate rule: declare-fields must name their anchor artifact
layered-model assessment (schemas as layer 2, runtime-coupled state as layer 3) -- holding as planning-surface vocabulary, not yet promoting to schema
in-session-capture-triggers cross-schema applicability -- narrative-only or surface from every schema's meta?
schema-to-harness cross-reference -- surface gate-layer contracts from schema meta so walls are discoverable from terrain
document Arc use-case explicitly in narrative schema meta -- currently lives only in pilot record and commit messages
.claude/settings.json reference in Getting-Started needs adoption-friendly wording -- portable set does not include it, but the example points at it

known-decisions.decision: terms-to-skills conversion rejected -- skills are optional by default and terms are load-bearing by reference, so converting would fragment a working cross-referencing ontology and lose the negation topology
known-decisions.requested-by: collaborating agent (initial proposal, walked back after reading live Workorders terms file)

known-decisions.decision: Vision split -- identity and locked invariants stay in AGENTS-Vision, longitudinal rationale (decisions and their why) moves to AGENTS-Arc -- Arc is the narrative schema applied to the product's own evolution, not a new schema
AGENTS-Arc chosen as the name for the longitudinal-rationale artifact -- story-arc shape fits the load and carries implicit permission for natural compression over time

BECAUSE precision bar adopted -- a rationale is durable when a future agent in a new session could cite it back to the current human and win the argument on the strength of the rationale alone -- recorded in Getting-Started-For-Humans.txt as canonical

guardrail-against-self recognized and preserved as a product feature/control mechanism -- the rationale record exists in part to hold a future human to the standard a past human set when the two disagree -- protects against the generous-mood-human plus agreeable-agent failure mode in both directions
AGENTS-Arc piloted with the narrative schema in AI-Links itself -- storage path narratives/projects/ai-links.json plus narratives/register.json -- JSON output per schema mandate

in-session-capture-triggers (render-inversion rule A, self-inspection digest rule B) added to narrative schema meta as terrain -- paired with a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json at repo root as the harness companion -- schema describes the shape, hook enforces at the edge of agent action -- shipped as the first worked example of the schemas-describe-harnesses-enforce separation

narrative schema promoted from reactive-stepchild to load-bearing upstream schema -- the promotion came from three concrete shifts: gained in-session triggers, got a harness companion, found a second use-case (Arc) -- not marketing, structural role change -- the narrative schema now governs rationale for changes to the other three schemas in any deployment that adopts the Arc pattern

what-done-looks-like-at-this-scope role text tightened in AGENTS-schema-governance.json -- role-text-only change chosen over a rename (rename would have broken gov2gov-resilience for existing deployments) -- new role text uses affirmation-only language: scope-level invariant that holds across the continuing session loop, durable target, delineated from session-close by naming the sibling field agent-session-open-close-measurement rather than by saying 'NOT a session-end criterion' -- negation-by-exclusion rejected as weaker terrain -- manifest bumped 0.1.4 to 0.1.5 to record the new governance sha256

good.description: pressure-test abstract ideas against a live artifact before committing to them -- the terms-to-skills proposal sounded coherent in the abstract and was defeated by ten minutes of reading the actual Workorders/AGENTS-Terms.md file -- any future schema-shape proposal should be evaluated against at least one live consuming artifact before it lands in a schema edit

name-with-load-attached -- when Mike asks the agent to name something (AGENTS-Arc, guardrail-against-self), the chosen name must carry the load the artifact does -- names that do not carry the load produce drift, names that do carry the load produce alignment -- the narrative schema output format choice (JSON not markdown) is an example: the name carried the constraint

ask for pushback when pushback is what is needed -- the explicit instruction 'Now is not the time to be overly agreeable' produced visibly different session output and caught at least two scope-creep moments that an agreeable agent would have ratified -- the instruction itself is a reusable capture pattern

bad.description: the glib BECAUSE -- rationale written as dismissal instead of precise reasoning fails the BECAUSE precision bar (a future agent could not cite it back and win the argument) -- the canonical example from this session was a shorthand 'skills sucks' framing used for the terms-to-skills rejection, which was later corrected (see entry e014) -- the actual defensible rationale is the one recorded in d001: skills are optional by default and designed for capability discovery, terms are load-bearing by reference and depend on block-read cross-references, so converting fragments the ontology -- the pattern captured here is the shorthand, not any stance on skills as a feature -- skills are good, just not the right tool for terms specifically

agent-in-front-of-generous-mood-human reopens closed decisions -- the agent does not know the decision was closed for a specific reason, the human has lost the reason, the reframe sounds reasonable and the direction quietly reverses -- this failure is what guardrail-against-self / durable BECAUSE clauses exist to prevent -- recorded so the pattern stays visible

shipment-session enforcement gap -- when a schema rule is added in the same session in which it becomes shippable, the paired harness hook does not fire until the next session starts -- this creates a window in which the rule is in effect but unenforced, and the agent that authored the rule must self-enforce it on the very next turn -- observed this session: A+B shipped, next turn rendered a four-item list without materializing to Arc, Mike caught it before the agent did -- the pattern is not an argument against shipping rules and hooks together, it is a reminder that the authoring session is the hardest session to follow the rule in, because the agent's attention is on shipment not on the rule's behavioral demand

handoff-note: next session should read this Arc record before reading the scratchpads or pen test -- the scratchpads were inputs to the decisions recorded here, not replacements for them -- four threads are open (recovery-vs-continuation audit, TheLinks sync, model-narration integration, layered-model articulation) -- the open threads are parked intentionally, not forgotten; front-load them in the next session's open state -- the terms-to-skills walk-back is the canonical example of why schema-shape proposals must be pressure-tested against a live artifact before landing -- 'skills sucks' is the canonical example of a BECAUSE clause that fails the precision bar, kept in observed-patterns.bad for that reason
